stage-intentionally.label: Stage One Purpose at a Time
stage-intentionally.summary: Separate two supplied community-project purposes into focused snapshots.

sketch0.title: One Project Can Hold Several Purposes

sketch1.title: Stage the First Purpose by Name

try_stage_intentionally_sketch0.title: Stage Only the Schedule Purpose
try_stage_intentionally_sketch0.prompt:
A focused commit should contain one clear purpose. Use `git add schedule.txt` to stage only the schedule update. The contact change must remain unstaged in the working tree.

After staging, the proposed snapshot should contain only the change to `schedule.txt`. Do not commit it yet.

help.concept: `git add schedule.txt` copies only the current schedule into the staging area. The contact edit stays unstaged, allowing the two purposes to become separate commits.
help.hint_2: After staging, short status would show one staged edit and one unstaged edit.

try_stage_intentionally_sketch0.starterCode:
# Community Project

Shared community activities and updates.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf -- community-project
mkdir -p -- community-project
git -C community-project init -q -b main
git -C community-project config --local user.name 'ZoeSkoul Learner'
git -C community-project config --local user.email learner@zoeskoul.local
find community-project -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 ! -name .git -exec rm -rf -- {} +
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s '# Community Project

Shared community activities and updates.
' > community-project/README.md
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Community events

Saturday cleanup: 10:00 AM
' > community-project/schedule.txt
git -C community-project add -A
GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z git -C community-project commit -q -m 'Start community project'
find community-project -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 ! -name .git -exec rm -rf -- {} +
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s '# Community Project

Shared community activities and updates.
' > community-project/README.md
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Community contacts

Help desk: 555-0100
' > community-project/contacts.txt
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Community events

Saturday cleanup: 10:00 AM
' > community-project/schedule.txt
git -C community-project add -A
GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-02T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-02T12:00:00Z git -C community-project commit -q -m 'Add community contacts'
find community-project -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 ! -name .git -exec rm -rf -- {} +
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s '# Community Project

Shared community activities and updates.
' > community-project/README.md
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Community contacts

Help desk: 555-0100
' > community-project/contacts.txt
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Community events

Saturday cleanup: 10:00 AM
' > community-project/schedule.txt
git -C community-project add -A
find community-project -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 ! -name .git -exec rm -rf -- {} +
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s '# Community Project

Shared community activities and updates.
' > community-project/README.md
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Community contacts

Help desk: 555-0130
' > community-project/contacts.txt
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Community events

Saturday cleanup: 9:30 AM
' > community-project/schedule.txt

try_stage_intentionally_sketch0.instructions: Two unrelated edits are present: the cleanup time in `schedule.txt` and the help-desk number in `contacts.txt`. Stage only `schedule.txt` so the first proposed snapshot contains one purpose.

0.message: That action can discard or rewrite history. Use the safe, layer-specific workflow taught in this course.
Did you stage only the schedule change?

try_stage_intentionally_sketch1.title: Review the Proposed Schedule Snapshot
try_stage_intentionally_sketch1.prompt:
Inspect only the staged change in `schedule.txt` before committing. The unstaged edit in `contacts.txt` must remain outside this comparison.

Do not create the commit yet.

help.concept: `git diff --staged -- schedule.txt` compares the staged schedule with the current commit. `--staged` is essential here because ordinary `git diff` reads unstaged work instead.
help.hint_1: The output should show only the cleanup-time change.

0.message: That action can discard or rewrite history. Use the safe, layer-specific workflow taught in this course.
Did you review the staged schedule snapshot?

try_stage_intentionally_sketch2.title: Commit the Schedule Separately
try_stage_intentionally_sketch2.prompt:
Now record the staged schedule update with the exact commit message `Update community schedule`.

Because only `schedule.txt` is staged, the commit will not include the unrelated change in `contacts.txt`.

After committing, inspect the short status. Confirm that only the unstaged contact edit remains unfinished, proving that Git committed only the prepared schedule change.
try_stage_intentionally_sketch2.hint: Commit the current staging area, then inspect what remains.

help.concept: `git commit -m "Update community schedule"` records only the staged schedule. `git status --short` then reveals the still-unstaged contact edit, proving the commit boundary stayed focused.
help.hint_1: Do not stage `contacts.txt` before this commit.
help.hint_2: The repository should gain one commit and retain one unstaged edit.

try_stage_intentionally_sketch2.starterCode:
# Community Project

Shared community activities and updates.
try_stage_intentionally_sketch2.solutionCode:
git commit -m "Update community schedule"
git status --short

0.message: That action can discard or rewrite history. Use the safe, layer-specific workflow taught in this course.

1.message: Did you verify that the contact edit remains unstaged?

try_stage_intentionally_sketch3.title: Record the Contact Change Separately
try_stage_intentionally_sketch3.prompt:
Stage only `contacts.txt`, inspect its staged change, and then create a commit with the exact message `Update community contacts`.

When you finish, the contact update should be recorded in its own commit.
try_stage_intentionally_sketch3.hint: Prepare, inspect, and commit the remaining single-purpose change.

help.concept: `git add contacts.txt` stages the remaining edit. `git diff --staged -- contacts.txt` reviews the proposal, and the commit records it as a separate history entry.
help.hint_1: The staged diff should contain only the phone-number update.
help.hint_2: The final repository state should be clean.

try_stage_intentionally_sketch3.starterCode:
# Community Project

Shared community activities and updates.
try_stage_intentionally_sketch3.solutionCode:
git add contacts.txt
git diff --staged -- contacts.txt
git commit -m "Update community contacts"

single-choice-1.title: Choose a Focused Snapshot
single-choice-1.prompt: Two unrelated files are modified. What best keeps the next commit focused?
single-choice-1.hint: A focused commit starts by staging only the file or files that serve the same purpose.

help.concept: Build the next snapshot one purpose at a time, review the staged version, and reverse a staging decision without losing work.
help.hint_1: Remove options that act on a different repository layer than the one named in the question.
help.hint_2: Choose the answer that remains accurate even when other unrelated files exist.

options.a: Stage only the changes that serve one shared purpose.
options.d: To list the recorded commit history

multi-choice-1.title: A Coherent Snapshot
multi-choice-1.prompt: Which practices help keep a commit focused? Select all that apply.
multi-choice-1.hint: Judge each option independently; choose only the statements that stay true in this exact repository state.

help.concept: Build the next snapshot one purpose at a time, review the staged version, and reverse a staging decision without losing work.
help.hint_1: A partly true option is still wrong if it adds an effect Git does not guarantee.
help.hint_2: Use the layer named in the prompt—working tree, staging area, or history—to test each choice.

options.a: Stage files that serve the same project result.
options.b: Review the staged snapshot before committing.
options.c: Stage every changed file so status becomes quiet.
options.d: Use a subject that describes the result.

multi-choice-2.title: Inspect the Staged Boundary
multi-choice-2.prompt: Which commands can help verify what is staged?
multi-choice-2.hint: Choose the one answer that describes this command or concept precisely.

help.concept: Build the next snapshot one purpose at a time, review the staged version, and reverse a staging decision without losing work.
help.hint_1: Remove choices that act on a different repository layer or promise behavior Git does not provide.
help.hint_2: Prefer the exact definition used in this chapter over a nearby Git idea.

fill-blank-1.title: Write a Focused Commit Subject
fill-blank-1.prompt: The staged change expands the community cleanup time. Complete the subject: `git commit -m "[blank1]"`.
fill-blank-1.hint: Choose the term or symbol that matches the exact state described, not a nearby Git concept.

help.concept: Build the next snapshot one purpose at a time, review the staged version, and reverse a staging decision without losing work.
help.hint_1: Pay attention to whether the prompt describes an untracked path, a working-tree edit, staged work, or history.
help.hint_2: Read spaces, punctuation, and status columns as meaningful when the question shows Git output.

stage-intentionally-sketch0.title: Choose One Purpose
stage-intentionally-sketch0.bodyMarkdown: Two unrelated edits are currently present: the cleanup time changed in `schedule.txt`, and the help-desk number changed in `contacts.txt`.

stage-intentionally-sketch1.title: Review the Proposed Snapshot
stage-intentionally-sketch1.bodyMarkdown:
You have learned that `git diff --staged -- schedule.txt` shows the staged change that the next commit would record for `schedule.txt`.

This differs from ordinary `git diff`, which shows changes that are still unstaged. The `--staged` option changes which repository layer Git compares.

stage-intentionally-sketch2.title: Commit Only What Is Staged
stage-intentionally-sketch2.bodyMarkdown: Only the staged schedule update belongs in the next commit. The unrelated contact edit must remain in the working tree.

stage-intentionally-sketch3.title: Finish the Second Purpose
stage-intentionally-sketch3.bodyMarkdown: Apply the same focused workflow to the remaining contact update.
